one_time_description: Prior period adjustments: consolidation of Lionel Head Memorial Store, reclassification of portfolio investments to cost basis, and correction of deferred revenue error
economic_notes: First Nation is in Level Two ISC intervention due to financial and non-financial deficiencies. Recipient Appointed Advisor working with Council. COVID-19 impacts noted as limited for fiscal 2020. Operates Lionel Head Memorial Store through Red Earth Cree Developments Inc.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (year ended March 31, 2020). Prior year (2019) comparative figures were restated due to three prior period adjustments: (1) Lionel Head Memorial Store reclassified from modified equity to full consolidation, (2) Prince Albert Development Corporation and PANFBDLP investments reclassified to cost from modified equity reducing accumulated surplus by ~$1.87M, (3) $44,563 IELCC revenue correction to deferred revenue. First Nation is in ISC Level Two intervention. Consolidated entities include Red Earth Cree Nation Health Services, Section 95 Social Housing Operation, and Red Earth Cree Developments Inc. (Lionel Head Memorial Store). Restricted cash includes $7,028,653 capital project funds and $578,807 CMHC reserves. CMHC reserves underfunded by $1,332,521. Total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $14,563,575 + FNIHB $586,214 + CMHC $361,006 + Health Canada $54,983 + Emergency management $111,000 + PAGC $2,820,246 = note PAGC may be partially non-government; used sum of clearly government sources. expenses_housing combines Housing and Capital ($823,061) and CMHC Rental Housing ($559,697). No remuneration schedule included in provided text. Budget accumulated surplus shown as $33,312,418 vs actual $37,652,947.
